definition: A structure mapping positions to values for direct indexed access.
A structure representing ordered traversal and sequence.
A list implemented through nodes connected by references.
A last-in-first-out structure for nested contexts and backtracking.
A first-in-first-out structure for service order and workflow.
A double-ended queue allowing insertion and removal at both ends.
A fixed-capacity buffer that wraps around with modular indexing.
Add an item to the top of a stack.
Remove the most recently pushed item.
Add an item to the back of a queue.
Remove the oldest item from the front of a queue.
A condition where a bounded structure has no room for a new item.
A condition that must remain true for the structure to behave correctly.
